title: Lithium chloride purification of RNA from DSS-treated tissues
author: Emilie Viennois
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kk3cuyn

steps.instruction: In order to be purified from all polysaccharides including DSS, a purification using lithium chloride is performed. First, incubate the RNA with 0.1 volume of 8 M LiCl diluted in RNase-free water on ice for 2 h. (1/2) 02:00:00, Incubation on ice
Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 30 min at 4°C. (1/2) 00:30:00, Centrifugation
Pour the supernatants out and dissolve the pellet of RNA in 200 µL of RNase-free water. (1/2) undetermined, RNase-free water
Incubate the RNA with 0.1 volume of 8 M LiCl diluted in RNase-free water on ice for 2 h. (2/2) 02:00:00, Incubation
Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 30 min at 4°C. (2/2) 00:30:00, Centrifugation
Pour the supernatants out and dissolve the pellet of RNA in 200 µL of RNase-free water. (2/2) undetermined, RNase-free water
Precipitate the RNA at − 20°C for 30 min, in 0.1 volume of 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 2 volumes of 100% absolute ethanol. 00:30:00, Precipitation
Centrifuge the RNA at 14,000 g for 30 min at 4°C. 00:30:00, Centrifugation
Pur the supernatants out and wash the pellets with 100 μL of 70% ethanol. undetermined, 70% ethanol
Centrifuge at 14,000 g for 10 min at 4°C. 00:10:00, Centrifugation
Remove the supernatants and dissolve the RNA in 20– 50 μ l of RNAse-free water. undetermined, RNAse-free water
